scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE District Court / Civil Court
AT Bengaluru

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Ram Kumar,
Aged about 45 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Jayanagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Suresh Patel,
Aged about 38 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Koramangala, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY LENT / ADVANCE PAID WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at Bengaluru, and the Defendant carries on business / resides within such territorial limits.

3. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court.

LIMITATION

4. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 19 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from when the loan is made. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

5. The Defendant is known to the Plaintiff. On 15.03.2024, the Defendant requested the Plaintiff for a hand loan of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) for his personal requirements. Trusting the Defendant, the Plaintiff agreed to advance the said amount.

6. In pursuance of the said request, the Plaintiff advanced the sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- to the Defendant on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer. The transaction is evidenced by the bank record bearing UTR No. AXIB20240315123456. The proof of the said transfer is marked as Annexure A.

7. There was no written agreement executed between the parties regarding the loan. However, the Defendant acknowledged the receipt of the loan amount and his liability to repay the same via WhatsApp messages exchanged with the Plaintiff. The copies of the said digital communications are marked as Annexure B.

BREACH AND DEFAULT

8. The Plaintiff repeatedly made oral demands upon the Defendant to repay the loan amount. The Defendant failed to repay the amount by 20.06.2024, which is treated as the date of default for the purpose of interest calculation.

9. Consequently, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 to the Defendant calling upon him to repay the outstanding principal amount along with interest. The copy of the said legal notice is marked as Annexure C.

10. Despite receipt of the said legal notice and repeated requests, the Defendant has failed and neglected to repay the loan amount or any part thereof. The cause of action for the present suit arose on 20.06.2024 when the default occurred, within the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court.

INTEREST CLAIM

11. The Plaintiff claims interest on the principal amount at the rate of 12% per annum from the date of default i.e., 20.06.2024 till the date of realisation of the dues, as per prevailing legal norms.

12. The Defendant is liable to pay the principal sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- along with accrued interest and the costs of this suit. The total claim is valued for the purpose of court fee and jurisdiction as stated in the valuation section hereinbelow.

LEGAL BASIS

13. The present cause is governed by Indian Contract Act, 1872, including Section 73.

14. The present cause is governed by Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, including Section 9, Section 20, and Section 34.

CAUSE OF ACTION

15. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 20.06.2024 when the Defendant committed the breach / wrongful act giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to institute the present suit.

16. The cause of action further arose when the Defendant, despite receipt of the legal notice dated 01.07.2024, failed and refused to comply with the Plaintiff's demand.

17. The cause of action is not a continuing cause of action. The breach consisted of a single event. However, the Plaintiff's right to damages subsists.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

18. The suit is valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Court fee of Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- has been paid on this plaint under the provisions of the Court Fees Act, 1870 as applicable in the State of Karnataka. The plaint is properly stamped.

INTEREST

19. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pendente lite interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of filing of this suit till the date of judgment / decree, und
